articles.title: A TECHNICAL GUIDE TO BUILDING AGENTIC SYSTEMS
articles.summary: The next evolution in AI isn't better chat, it's agents that can actually do things: query databases, update systems, and make decisions. But connecting AI to every tool means custom code for each integration.

articles.title: Download the free whitepaper
SAM ALTMAN ON OPENAI'S REVENUE
articles.summary: Sam Altman revealed OpenAI's annual revenue run rate surpassed $20B, with future infrastructure commitments reaching $1.4 trillion. Plans include enterprise offerings, AI cloud services, and ventures into consumer devices and scientific discovery.

articles.title: OPENAI READIES GPT-5.1 THINKING MODEL AHEAD OF GEMINI 3 PRO
articles.summary: GPT-5.1 Thinking has been spotted in a backend component responsible for powering advanced reasoning processes within ChatGPT, indicating a GPT-5.1 release is close. Rumors point to a lineup of three models (Mini, Thinking, and a possible Codex-focused upgrade), each designed to fit different user needs and compute constraints. OpenAI is likely aiming to synchronize the release with Google's upcoming Gemini 3 Pro launch.

articles.title: $350B
articles.summary: Google plans to invest even more money into Anthropic. It is now in talks for a new funding round that could value Anthropic at $350 billion. The new deal could involve Google providing additional cloud computing services to Anthropic, a convertible note, or a priced funding round early next year. Google last month announced that it had inked a deal worth tens of billions for Anthropic to access Google's AI compute.

articles.title: FORECASTS OF AI & ECONOMIC GROWTH
articles.summary: Most economists forecast 0.1-1.5% annual excess growth through 2035, while AI insiders predict 3-30%. Most economists, though, assume that today's ChatGPT is essentially the ceiling of AI progress and that visions of a virtual AI coworker won't really materialize, but how is it possible that cheap, PhD-level intelligence wouldn't boost productivity? The forecasts imply that either AGI will be far less general than claimed or that adoption will be dramatically slower than historical technology diffusion. AI's 2024 economic footprint appears to be around 0.5% of GDP.

articles.title: 5 THOUGHTS ON KIMI K2 THINKING
articles.summary: Kimi K2 Thinking, a reasoning MoE model developed by China's Moonshot AI team, shows just how many people are learning quickly to train excellent AI models. Early reports note a distinctive style and writing quality - the model is beating leading closed models on some benchmarks such as Humanity's Last Exam and BrowseComp. It is the closest open models have been to the closed frontier of performance ever.

articles.title: Identity Challenge_
KIMI K2 THINKING
articles.summary: Chinese lab Moonshot AI released an open-source reasoning model that matches or exceeds GPT-5 and Claude Sonnet 4.5 on some benchmarks, including a 44.9% on Humanity's Last Exam and 71.3% on SWE-Bench Verified.

articles.title: GEMINI FILE SEARCH TOOL
articles.summary: Google introduced File Search, a built-in RAG system for the Gemini API that simplifies retrieval tasks. It includes vector search, automatic citations, and broad file format support with free storage and on-demand embedding.

articles.title: ACCUMULATING CONTEXT CHANGES THE BELIEFS OF LANGUAGE MODELS
articles.summary: Extended conversations can reshape how AI models answer the same question. After 10 rounds of debate about moral dilemmas, GPT-5 reversed its position 54.7% of the time when asked again. When models changed their stated beliefs, they also chose different tools to accomplish tasks.

articles.title: LEAVING META AND PYTORCH
articles.summary: Soumith Chintala has left Meta after working at the company for 11 years. Chintala spent almost eight years leading PyTorch, taking it from nothing to more than 90% adoption in AI. He plans to move to something small and new. PyTorch has been left in capable hands, and Chintala is confident that its values will stay intact, but things may change in flavor because he will no longer impose his own taste from the top.

articles.title: GOOGLE DEPLOYS NEW AXION CPUS AND SEVENTH-GEN IRONWOOD TPU — TRAINING AND INFERENCING PODS BEAT NVIDIA GB300 AND SHAPE 'AI HYPERCOMPUTER' MODEL
articles.summary: Google Cloud has introduced new AI-oriented instances powered by its own Axion CPUs and Ironwood TPUs. The new instances are aimed at both training and low-latency inference. Servers running these chips come equipped with Google's custom Titanium-branded controllers, which offload network, security, and I/O storage processing, enabling better management and higher performance. More details about Google's in-house designed processors are available in the article.

ELON MUSK SAYS TESLA NEEDS TO BUILD ‘GIGANTIC CHIP FAB' TO MEET AI AND ROBOTICS NEEDS
articles.summary: Tesla doesn't yet manufacture its own microchips, but it has been designing custom chips for autonomous driving for several years.

articles.title: INTRODUCING PARALLEL SEARCH: THE HIGHEST ACCURACY WEB SEARCH API ENGINEERED FOR AI
articles.summary: The Parallel Search API is a web search tool designed for AI agents engineered to deliver the most relevant, token-efficient web data at the lowest cost.

articles.title: MICROSOFT FORMS SUPERINTELLIGENCE TEAM UNDER AI CHIEF SULEYMAN ‘TO SERVE HUMANITY'
articles.summary: In a thinly veiled dig at other AI labs, Mustafa Suleyman said the team will focus on practical technologies—like digital companions, disease diagnostics, and renewable energy—rather than “ill-defined and ethereal superintelligence."

articles.title: ERIC ZELIKMAN, A TOP AI RESEARCHER WHO DEPARTED XAI IN SEPTEMBER, IS RAISING $1 BILLION AT A $4 BILLION VALUATION FOR HIS NEW STARTUP
articles.summary: Eric Zelikman, who recently left xAI, is raising $1 billion for his startup Humans& at a $4 billion valuation.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY UPGRADED COMET ASSISTANT
articles.summary: Perplexity's Comet Assistant has been redesigned to handle more complex, multi-tab tasks with improved web environment interaction.
